news.title: Jamie Dimon Says JPMorgan Stock Isn’t Cheap. Why the Bank Is Still Buying Back Billions.
news.summary: CEO Jamie Dimon says  JPMorgan  Chase’s stock isn’t cheap, but that hasn’t stopped the company from buying back $32 billion of stock over the past four quarters.  The bank has one of the larger share repurchase programs in the country, and uses more than half its annual earnings for stock buybacks.  There’s reason for JPMorgan to rein in its buybacks while the stock trades at lofty multiples—for the benefit of the bank itself and its shareholders.

news.title: Jamie Dimon Sells $40 Million of JPMorgan Stock
news.summary: JPMorgan Chief Executive Jamie Dimon sold more than $40 million of the bank’s stock on Wednesday in the latest cash out of his shares.  Dimon has run the bank for over two decades and didn’t start selling any of his bank stocks until the end of 2023 when he sold a million shares worth around $180 million.  Dimon, his wife and his family trusts hold the shares.

source_event.title: Mortgage rates dip to 4-week low — just in time for the best week of the year to sell a home

signals.summary: FCC Chair approves Amazon's acquisition of Globalstar for satellite internet expansion, signaling regulatory support for competition against SpaceX in space-based connectivity.

affected_positions.rationale: FCC approval accelerates Amazon's satellite internet strategy, expanding competitive positioning in growing space-connectivity market against SpaceX.

source_event.title: FCC Chair Brendan Carr Gives Nod To Amazon-Globalstar Deal As Satellite Internet Race With Elon Musk-Led SpaceX Intensifies: 'We're Very Open-Minded…'

signals.summary: ExxonMobil and partners announce plans to begin deepwater drilling offshore Greece in 2027, targeting new hard-to-reach oil and gas reserves in a frontier exploration region.

affected_positions.rationale: Deepwater exploration project expands reserve potential and geographic diversification, though material production is years away and execution risk remains elevated.

source_event.title: ExxonMobil & Partners Plan to Begin Deepwater Drilling Offshore Greece

signals.summary: Airline stocks rallied on oil pullback following ceasefire news, but analysts warn the relief may be temporary due to ongoing headline risk and earnings uncertainty.

affected_positions.rationale: Oil pullback creates short-term downward pressure on energy sector revenues and margins.

source_event.title: Airline stocks rebounded as oil pulled back. Why the rally may not last

signals.summary: Escalating Middle East conflict with Iran closing Strait of Hormuz and failed ceasefire talks is causing extreme oil price volatility. Article suggests pivot away from integrated majors like Exxon toward upstream land/royalty plays.

affected_positions.rationale: Article explicitly recommends moving away from Exxon despite oil volatility, suggesting better opportunities exist elsewhere in the energy value chain.
Geopolitical crisis with naval blockades and failed peace talks drives safe-haven demand and inflation hedging needs.
High-beta risk asset typically sells off during acute geopolitical crises when investors flee to traditional safe havens.
Flight-to-quality during Middle East military escalation benefits long-duration Treasuries despite inflation concerns from oil shock.
Consumer discretionary faces margin pressure from energy cost spike and potential demand destruction if oil shock persists.

source_event.title: Forget Exxon. The Smartest Oil Trade in 2026 Is the Company That Owns the Ground Beneath It

signals.summary: TeraWulf, a Bitcoin mining company, is raising $900M to expand into AI data centers, signaling sector diversification away from pure crypto mining into higher-margin AI infrastructure.

affected_positions.rationale: TeraWulf's pivot to AI suggests Bitcoin mining margins remain compressed, but does not directly impact BTC price or adoption fundamentals.
Expanding AI data center infrastructure validates continued enterprise AI demand, indirectly supporting cloud and AI platform providers like Microsoft.

source_event.title: TeraWulf To Raise $900 Million For A.I. Data Centre Expansion
